recalls.Description:
GLMZZ has issued a recall for its Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Sets, which include eight colorful spherical magnets. Each ball measures approximately 1.24 inches in diameter and is packaged in a black zippered case. These toys pose a significant risk, particularly to children, due to the presence of high-powered magnets that can be ingested. When swallowed, these magnets can attract one another or metal objects, leading to severe internal injuries such as intestinal perforations, blockages, or even death.

Consumers are urged to cease use of the recalled toys immediately and keep them away from children. GLMZZ is offering a full refund for the returned products, and customers will need to dispose of the toys safely. To complete the refund process, consumers should send a photo of the disposed toys to GLMZZ via email.
recalls.Title: GLMZZ Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Sets Recalled Due to Serious Injury Risks from Magnet Ingestion

Products.Name: GLMZZ Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Set

Images.Caption: Recalled GLMZZ Fidget Magnet Ball Toys
Recalled GLMZZ Fidget Magnet Ball Toys (In Small Parts Test Cylinder)

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from February 2025 to October 2025 for about $15.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Jiaying E-commerce Co., Ltd., dba GLMZZ, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion due to high-powered magnets, leading to severe internal injuries.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys, keep them away from children, and contact GLMZZ for a full refund.

recalls.metaDescription: GLMZZ recalls Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Sets due to serious ingestion risks. Immediate action required for refund and safety.

recalls.Description: TheKiddoSpace is recalling its LED soccer hover balls due to a serious burn hazard. These black and white plastic balls, featuring foam bumpers, are marked with the logo "HOVER LED SOCCER BALL" and the phrase "GLIDE INTO FUN!" on their packaging. This recall specifically affects the smaller hover balls measuring approximately 5.5 inches in diameter.
recalls.Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls LED Soccer Hover Balls Due to Burn Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com or visit their website at www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/soccer-hover-ball. Alternatively, visit www.thekiddospacestore.com and click on "Product Safety Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled TheKiddoSpace LED Soccer Hover Ball

Injuries.Name: TheKiddoSpace has received three reports of a burning smell coming from the hover ball when in use. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Received by consumers as promotional giveaways from December 2024 through February 2025.

Importers.Name: SN Commerce LLC, dba TheKiddoSpaceStore, of Dover, Delaware

Hazards.Name: Excessive surface temperature of batteries, posing a burn injury risk.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the recalled hover balls immediately, keep them away from children, and contact TheKiddoSpace for a free replacement. They will need to mark the ball as "RECALLED" with permanent marker, send a photo of it to info@thekiddospace.com, and dispose of the ball according to local waste guidelines.

recalls.metaDescription: TheKiddoSpace recalls LED soccer hover balls due to burn hazards. Stop using them and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description: TheKiddoSpace is recalling its reusable water balloons due to potential injury risks associated with phthalate exposure. These refillable, multi-colored silicone water balloons feature small magnets to keep them sealed when filled and are packaged in a black mesh storage bag. The product's resealable blue packaging displays the TheKiddoSpace logo, the words "Reusable Water Balloons," and an image of a child holding a water balloon.
recalls.Title: TheKiddoSpace Recalls Reusable Water Balloons Due to Phthalate Exposure Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, consumers can reach TheKiddoSpace via email at info@thekiddospace.com or visit www.thekiddospacestore.com/pages/product-recalls/water-balloons. Alternatively, consumers can go to www.thekiddospacestore.com and click on "Product Safety Recalls" at the bottom of the page.

Retailers.Name: Online at TheKiddoSpaceStore.com from April 2024 through February 2025 for between $15 and $30.

Importers.Name: SN Commerce LLC, dba TheKiddoSpaceStore, of Dover, Delaware

Hazards.Name: The storage bags for the water balloons contain a regulated phthalate, violating federal regulations. Phthalates can be harmful if ingested by young children, leading to serious health issues.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should immediately stop using the recalled water balloons and remove the storage bag from children's access. They can contact TheKiddoSpace for a free replacement bag, including shipping. To process the replacement, consumers must cut the storage bag in half and send a photo of the destroyed bag to info@thekiddospace.com. The destroyed bags should be disposed of according to local waste disposal regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: TheKiddoSpace recalls reusable water balloons due to phthalate exposure risks. Immediate action required for consumer safety.

recalls.Description: Based Online is recalling its Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Sets due to safety concerns. Each set contains four textured, silicone-covered magnet balls in colors including white, yellow, blue, and purple, measuring approximately 1.24 inches in diameter. These toys are packaged in a black fabric zippered case and lack any identifying marks. The presence of loose high-powered magnets in these toys violates mandatory toy safety standards, creating a serious ingestion hazard for children. If swallowed, these magnets can attract each other or other metal objects, potentially causing severe internal injuries, including intestinal perforations, blockages, blood poisoning, and even death.
recalls.Title: Recall of Based Online Fidget Magnet Ball Toys Due to Serious Injury or Death Risk from Magnet Ingestion
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled toys immediately, keep them away from children, and contact Based Online for a full refund. Dispose of the toys and email a photo of the disposal to Based Online at recall@mail.goclimber.cn.

Products.Name: Based Online Fidget Magnet Ball Toy Set

Images.Caption: Recalled Based Online Fidget Magnet Ball Toys

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from February 2025 through October 2025 for about $6.

Distributors.Name: Xiaofei He, dba Based Online, of China

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to loose high-powered magnets

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Based Online Fidget Magnet Ball Toys due to ingestion risks. Immediate action required for safety.

recalls.Description: A recall has been issued for children's spiral tower toys that pose a serious choking risk. These toys feature a three-tiered design with a basketball hoop and a character's face at the top, measuring approximately 10 inches in height and 4 inches in width. Each toy comes with three small yellow balls, which are a violation of the small ball ban for toys intended for children under three years old.
recalls.Title: Recall of Children's Spiral Tower Toys Due to Choking Hazard; Sold by STWUQIKONG on Shein
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, contact STWUQIKONG via email at STWUQIKONG_RECALL@hotmail.com.

Distributors.Name: Shantou Jinping District Wuqikong Daily Necessities Store, dba STWUQIKONG, of China

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to small balls; violates small ball ban

Remedies.Name: Return the toy for a full refund; dispose of the product and send a photo to STWUQIKONG_RECALL@hotmail.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Children's Spiral Tower Toys recalled due to choking hazard. Contact STWUQIKONG for refunds and disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: TEMI Toys has issued a recall for their Go Basketball children's toys due to a serious choking risk. This toy features five colorful levels and a basketball hoop adorned with a character's face, measuring approximately 13 inches tall and 4 inches wide. It includes three yellow balls and is labeled with "TEMI GO BASKETBALL" on the packaging. The toy is not suitable for children under three years old, violating safety regulations concerning small balls.
recalls.Title: TEMI Go Basketball Toys Recalled Due to Choking Hazard for Young Children
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should reach out to TEMI Toys via email at Temitoys@outlook.com.

Retailers.Name: Amazon from September 2024 through April 2025 for between $10 and $18.

Distributors.Name: Shan tou shi bei bei jia mao yi you xian gong si dba TEMI Toys, of China

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to small balls; violates small ball ban for children under three.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the toy immediately and contact TEMI Toys for a full refund. They will need to dispose of the toy and provide a photo of the disposed item to Temitoys@outlook.com.

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: TEMI Go Basketball Toys pose a choking hazard for children under three. Contact TEMI Toys for a refund.

recalls.Description: Petmate is recalling its Pet Zone-branded Laser Pointer and LED Ball pet toys due to a significant safety risk. These toys, which come with six preinstalled button cell batteries, include a laser pointer featuring blue paw prints and measuring approximately 2.9 inches in length. The product is marked with the words "Danger" and "Accession No. 2430278-000" on a silver label on the laser pointer tube. The LED ball, which is clear and about 1.6 inches in diameter, is also part of this recall. The packaging displays the "PET ZONE" logo along with the product name and the item number "Item #:15712EAM" on the back.
recalls.Title: Petmate Recalls Pet Zone Laser Pointer & LED Ball Pet Toys Due to Serious Injury or Death Risk from Ingestion Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can reach Petmate at their toll-free number 888-847-8716, available from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, visit https://recallrtr.com/pettoys or https://petmate.com and click on "Recall" at the bottom of the page.

Products.Name: Pet Zone Laser Pointer & LED Ball Pet Toys

Hazards.Name: Ingestion hazard due to accessible button cell batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the toys and contact Petmate for a full refund

recalls.metaDescription: Petmate recalls Pet Zone Laser Pointer & LED Ball toys due to ingestion hazards. Contact for refunds and safety information.

recalls.Description: Hobby Toy Hammer Ball sets, model number 711, are being recalled due to a choking hazard that poses a serious risk of injury or death. These toy sets feature a base with buttons, a detachable door, four small basins, and a monkey sign. The green base is labeled "FOREST ROLLER BALL," while the brown gate reads "ROLLER BALL" and the hammer side states "FISHING." The dimensions of the sets are 15 x 10.6 x 13.2 inches and include a hammer along with four small colored balls measuring approximately 1.5 inches in diameter, available in yellow, light pink, dark pink, and blue.
recalls.Title: Hobby Toy Hammer Ball Sets Recalled for Choking Hazard Risk; Sold Exclusively on Amazon
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further information, reach out via email at haochenghengkj@outlook.com, visit www.hcfjehtoy.com, or go to www.amazon.com/stores/page/2912E5E1-628F-4384-930C-5D8CCB7B8184.

Distributors.Name: Shantou Haochengheng Technology Co. Ltd, of China

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to small balls, posing serious injury or death risk for children under three.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled toys immediately and contact Shantou Haochengheng Technology for instructions on product destruction and full refund. Purchasers will also be contacted directly by Shantou Haochengheng Technology and Amazon.

recalls.Description: Twenty Four Six Foods has announced a recall of all flavors of Happiness USA liquid rolling candy, which includes blue raspberry, strawberry, and green apple varieties. Each bottle contains approximately one fluid ounce of flavored liquid candy and features a rolling ball applicator designed for tongue application. The product is packaged in a resealable bottle with a twist-off cap, and is labeled with "Funtime Roller Ball Candy" on the front and "Happiness USA" branding along with warning labels on the back.
recalls.Title: Twenty Four Six Foods Issues Recall for Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy Due to Choking Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should immediately cease use of the candy, remove it from children's reach, and contact Twenty Four Six Foods for a complete refund. For assistance, reach out to Twenty Four Six Foods at 833-436-1200 from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Thursday, or email recall246foods@gmail.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy (front)
Recalled Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy (back)
Recalled Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy (all flavors)

Injuries.Name: Twenty Four Six Foods has received one report of the rolling ball dislodging, resulting in a seven-year-old child choking in New Jersey in July 2023. The child underwent emergency surgery to successfully remove the rolling ball from his throat.

Retailers.Name: Kosher market stores nationwide from January 2018 through July 2023 for about $2.

Importers.Name: Twenty Four Six Foods LLC, of Brooklyn, New York

Hazards.Name: Choking hazard due to dislodging rolling ball

Remedies.Name: Stop using, remove from children, and contact for refund

recalls.metaDescription: Recall alert: Happiness USA Roller Ball Candy poses choking risks. Stop use and contact for a refund.
